title: Anatomy of a Metric Space
description: A flowchart illustrating the core definition of a metric space and how examples are derived from it.
elements: Four Axioms (Non-negativity, Identity, Symmetry, Triangle Inequality)
relationships: A Set X and a function d are the inputs
The function d must satisfy the four axioms to be a metric
The pair (X,d) that satisfies the axioms forms a Metric Space
Various specific examples are instances of this general definition
